ops.body:
RESOLVED 2026-07-18. Commit 3d55ae2e (drain D2/D4/D5) was suspected of a -race deadlock after macOS CI run 29624781344 was cancelled at the 10-min cap. CI-log triage exonerated drain (it passed in 1.184s in that run) and identified a recurring macOS-only hang across ≥5 runs on unrelated commits.

**True root cause (found by recovering the on-disk fix + local -race looping):** a wire.Server admission race, NOT pipe-holding children. `Server.dispatch` (classConcurrent) did a non-blocking `select { case s.queue <- j: default: reject }` against a Backlog-sized queue; with Backlog:0 the send needed a worker *instantaneously* ready, so on the slower macOS scheduler the request was spuriously rejected and TestConcurrentPoolRejectionProvesNonDispatch hung forever on <-started (the "wire package never prints" symptom). Two adjacent latent flakes surfaced under the same -race looping: TestShutdownSettlesQueuedWork dropped admitted work when a ctx-cancel enqueue escape raced shutdown, and TestTrustRejectionShortCircuits raced a close because the trust denial replied before reading the client's frame.

**Fix (commit 5e3c9ce4, CI green incl. macOS 42s):** slots admission counter (cap Workers+Backlog) decoupled from worker readiness + unconditional blocking enqueue (drains admitted work through shutdown); trust check moved after the frame read (delivers the rejection instead of racing a close); test hygiene (proc closefds → /dev/null + reap, trust E2E → t.Cleanup reap); harness default -timeout 600→480s so a future hang dumps goroutines before the job cap. Design invariant recorded in note 9e4994e. Proven: wire 30/30 + proc/trust 3/3 under -race, trust E2E plain+race green. Continued CI green is the ongoing confirmation.
